title: The Octopus Swordsman Presses Zoro
description: On pages 8–11, the octopus swordsman presses zoro focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.
scene: On pages 8–11, the octopus swordsman presses zoro focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The hell do you think you're playing at! Get your ass down this instant!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You trying to buy time or something!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I've heard that you use 3 swords.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So why are you only using one!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's because using one sword is still more than enough to beat you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Shut up! I haven't fought seriously yet!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Why Human Swords- Men can never hope to beat me.

dialogue.speaker: Hatchan
dialogue.text: Damn it! That octopus bastard is wasting time!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: But if we jump into the sea right now, he'll drag us under and drown us!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: At this rate, Brother Luffy's going to die...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Do you think you can really accomplish anything by jumping into the sea with those wounds of yours!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I'll go save that rubber boy instead.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: If we all go, it'll be easier for the fishmen to spot us and that'll only bring more trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: It'll be meaningless if we end up hindering their fight.

dialogue.speaker: Nojiko
dialogue.text: Nojiko...! But your stomach is still...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: But those guys are fighting for my sister's sake!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And the reason why humans can't beat me is...

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
